name.de: Tourte de Seigle (Roggenlaib aus der Auvergne)
name.es: Tourte de seigle (hogaza de centeno de Auvernia)

description.en: The flattish, rustic rye round of the Auvergne highlands, where rye long outperformed wheat on volcanic soil. Built on a firm rye levain and baked scorching hot, it is dense, deeply flavored, and built to last weeks, not days.
description.de: Der flache, rustikale Roggenlaib des Auvergne-Hochlands, wo Roggen dem Weizen auf vulkanischem Boden lange überlegen war. Auf einem festen Roggensauerteig aufgebaut und glühend heiß gebacken, ist er dicht, aromatisch und für Wochen statt Tage haltbar gemacht.
description.es: La hogaza de centeno rústica y achatada de las tierras altas de Auvernia, donde el centeno superó al trigo en el suelo volcánico. Con levain firme de centeno y horneada a fuego vivo, es densa, sabrosa y pensada para durar semanas, no días.
description.fr: La miche plate et rustique du plateau auvergnat, où le seigle a longtemps surpassé le blé sur les terres volcaniques. Bâtie sur un levain dur de seigle et cuite à four vif, elle est dense, pleine de goût, et faite pour se garder des semaines plutôt que des jours.

origin.note: Rye was historically the reliable grain of Auvergne's cold volcanic highlands, where wheat struggled; the tourte was baked large and kept for weeks between village bakes.

keeping.note: A dense wholegrain rye levain loaf that traditionally kept village families going for a week or two between bakes.

summary.en: A firm rye sourdough levain, built up over previous feeds, is the base of the dough.
Knead the rye flour, water, salt, levain and a touch of yeast about 8 minutes to a heavy, cohesive paste.
Divide into large pieces and shape into flattish rounds with only moderate tension.
Proof in floured bannetons about 35 minutes.
Bake at a fierce, rising heat for a long bake until the crust is thick and deeply set; cool fully on a rack.

source.adaptation: Firm levain's flour/water content estimated at roughly 2:1 flour:water and folded into the total baker's percentages.
